_comment: Source: Michigan EMS Scope of Practice — Michigan DHHS Bureau of Emergency Preparedness, EMS and Systems of Care (Updated 04/2024). Per MCL 333.20956: no provider shall practice at a level inconsistent with their education, licensure, and approved MCA protocols.
document_reference: Michigan EMS Scope of Practice — MDHHS Bureau of Emergency Preparedness, EMS and Systems of Care (Updated 04/2024)
legal_authority: MCL 333.20956: A MFR/EMR, EMT, AEMT, or Paramedic shall not provide life support at a level inconsistent with education, licensure, and approved MCA protocols.
note: Michigan scope expands on the NASEMSO national minimum in several areas (e.g., MFR/EMR NPA, i-gel, pulse ox, blood glucose, aspirin, oral glucose, traction splint). All interventions are subject to MCA protocol approval and medical director credentialing. Michigan uses 'Specialist/AEMT' for the AEMT level.

MFR.description: Michigan's first responder level, equivalent to the national EMR. Michigan MFR scope is expanded relative to the NASEMSO national minimum in several skills (NPA, i-gel, pulse oximetry, blood glucose, aspirin, oral glucose, traction splint).
michigan_expansions_vs_national: NPA (nasal airway) — authorized at MFR level in Michigan; national minimum is EMT
i-gel supraglottic airway — authorized at MFR level in Michigan; national minimum is EMT
Pulse oximetry — authorized at MFR level in Michigan; national minimum is EMT
Blood glucose monitoring — authorized at MFR level in Michigan; national minimum is EMT
Aspirin — authorized at MFR level in Michigan; national minimum is EMT
Oral glucose — authorized at MFR level in Michigan; national minimum is EMT
Cervical collar — authorized at MFR level in Michigan
Long spine board — authorized at MFR level in Michigan; national minimum is EMT
Traction splint — authorized at MFR level in Michigan; national minimum is EMT
Intramuscular auto-injector and draw-up epinephrine — MFR authorized (if MCA approved)
medication_routes: Intramuscular — auto-injector and draw-up epinephrine (if MCA approved)
medications: Auto-injectors: epinephrine, narcotic antagonist, HazMat exposure
other: Vital signs (BP manual, pulse, respiratory rate)

EMT.description: Michigan EMT. Builds on MFR with additional airway, monitoring, and medication capabilities. Includes supraglottic airway (King, Air-Qsp3/3G, LMA Supreme, Combitube), CPAP, 12-lead ECG acquisition.
airway_ventilation: Airway — supraglottic (King LT, Air-Qsp3 or 3G, Combitube, LMA Supreme)
End-tidal CO2 monitoring and waveform capnography interpretation (if MCA approved and trained)
Oxygen — humidifiers, partial rebreather, simple face mask, Venturi mask
cardiovascular: Telemetric monitoring devices and transmission of clinical data (including video)
medications: Beta-agonist bronchodilator (MDI or nebulized, including duoneb) for dyspnea/wheezing
Glucagon IM and/or IN (only with MCA approval)
Immunizations during a public health emergency
Oral OTC analgesics (acetaminophen, ibuprofen) for pain or fever
Sublingual nitroglycerin (patient's own prescribed and agency-stocked per MCA)
IV antibiotics for interfacility transports (hospital-established; only if MCA approved)
iv_fluids: IV maintenance — peripheral site (non-medicated, maintenance of existing IV access)

AEMT.description: Michigan AEMT, officially titled 'Emergency Medical Technician Specialist.' Adds IV/IO access initiation, IV medications, waveform capnography, and expanded medication formulary including antiemetics and parenteral analgesia (per Paramedic in Michigan — see note).
michigan_notes: Michigan AEMT does NOT include parenteral analgesia (morphine, fentanyl) — that is Paramedic-only in Michigan
Epinephrine 1:10 IV is authorized at AEMT level in Michigan
Antiemetics are authorized at AEMT level
Waveform capnography authorized at AEMT (and EMT if MCA approved)
airway_ventilation: End-tidal CO2 monitoring and interpretation of waveform capnography
Suctioning — tracheobronchial of intubated patient (Paramedic only per Michigan SOP — see note)
medications: IV antibiotics for interfacility transports (hospital-established, MCA approved)

Paramedic.description: Michigan Paramedic. Full ALS scope. Includes all skills of lower levels plus advanced airway (ETI, cricothyrotomy), manual defibrillation, cardioversion, transcutaneous pacing, RSI agents, vasopressors, antidysrhythmics, thrombolytics, and all medication routes.
airway_ventilation: Chest decompression — needle or commercial device
Airway obstruction — dislodgement by direct laryngoscopy
Oxygen therapy — high flow nasal cannula
cardiovascular: Transvenous cardiac pacing — monitoring and maintenance
Access indwelling catheters and implanted central IV ports
IV maintenance of medicated IV fluids
medications: Antiarrhythmics (amiodarone, lidocaine, adenosine, atropine, procainamide, magnesium)
Maintain infusion of blood or blood products
Parenteral analgesia for pain (morphine, fentanyl, ketorolac, ketamine)

key_michigan_differences_from_nasemso_na: MFR/EMR: i-gel authorized (national EMT minimum for supraglottic)
MFR/EMR: Pulse oximetry authorized (national EMT minimum)
MFR/EMR: Blood glucose monitoring authorized (national EMT minimum)
MFR/EMR: Oral glucose authorized (national EMT minimum)
MFR/EMR: Long spine board, traction splint authorized (national EMT minimum)
EMT: Sublingual nitroglycerin from agency stock authorized (national AEMT minimum for stock; national EMT only for patient's own Rx)
EMT: Glucagon IM/IN with MCA approval (national AEMT minimum)
EMT: End-tidal CO2 with MCA approval (national AEMT minimum for interpretation)
AEMT: Parenteral analgesia is NOT listed — Michigan places this at Paramedic level only
Paramedic: Michigan includes racemic epinephrine, tetracaine, antihemorrhagic agents explicitly
mca_selection_required_items: End-tidal CO2 waveform capnography at EMT level — requires MCA approval and training
CO2 colorimetric device at MFR level — requires MCA approval
Mechanical CPR device at MFR/EMT level — requires MCA approval
Active compression-decompression device — requires MCA approval
Glucagon IM at EMT level — requires MCA approval
Intramuscular auto-injector and draw-up epinephrine at MFR level — requires MCA approval
